80	  /** Optional override for batchCreateRotationsFn (used by cohost routes) */
81	  onBatchCreateRotations?: (args: {
82	    data: Record<string, unknown>
83	  }) => Promise<{
84	    success: boolean
85	    data: { rotationIds: string[]; rotations: unknown[] }
86	  }>
87	  /** Optional override for batchUpdateVolunteerRotationsFn (used by cohost routes) */
88	  onBatchUpdateVolunteerRotations?: (args: {
89	    data: Record<string, unknown>
90	  }) => Promise<{
91	    success: boolean
92	    data: { rotationIds: string[]; rotations: unknown[] }
93	  }>
94	}
95	
96	/**
97	 * Form schema for multiple rotations.
98	 * Judge selected once, multiple rotation blocks managed with useFieldArray.
99	 */
100	const multiRotationSchema = z.object({
101	  membershipId: z.string().min(1, "Judge is required"),
102	  rotations: z
103	    .array(
104	      z.object({
105	        startingHeat: z
106	          .number()
107	          .int()
108	          .min(1, "Starting heat must be at least 1"),
109	        startingLane: z
110	          .number()
111	          .int()
112	          .min(1, "Starting lane must be at least 1"),
113	        heatsCount: z.number().int().min(1, "Must cover at least 1 heat"),
114	        notes: z.string().max(500, "Notes too long").optional(),
115	      }),
116	    )
117	    .min(1, "At least one rotation required"),
118	})
119	
120	type MultiRotationFormValues = z.infer<typeof multiRotationSchema>
121	
122	/**
123	 * Multi-rotation editor component with accordion-style UI.
124	 * Allows creating/editing multiple rotations for the same volunteer.
125	 * Each rotation block is collapsible with independent form fields.
126	 * Preview cells for ALL blocks with different visual treatment per block.
127	 */
128	export function MultiRotationEditor({
129	  competitionId,
130	  teamId,
131	  trackWorkoutId,
132	  maxHeats,
133	  maxLanes,
134	  availableJudges,
135	  rotationsByVolunteer,
136	  existingRotations,
137	  initialHeat,
138	  initialLane,
139	  externalPosition,
140	  activeBlockIndex,
141	  onActiveBlockChange,
142	  eventLaneShiftPattern,
143	  eventDefaultHeatsCount,
144	  filterEmptyLanes,
145	  occupiedLanesByHeat,
146	  onSuccess,
147	  onCancel,
148	  onPreviewChange,
149	  onJudgeSelect,
150	  onBatchCreateRotations,
151	  onBatchUpdateVolunteerRotations,
152	}: MultiRotationEditorProps) {
153	  const isEditing = !!existingRotations && existingRotations.length > 0
154	  // When adding a new rotation (activeBlockIndex >= existingRotations length),
155	  // start with no blocks open - the new block will be opened when it's added
156	  // Otherwise, open the first block by default
157	  const isAddingNewRotation =
158	    isEditing && activeBlockIndex >= (existingRotations?.length ?? 0)
159	  const [openBlocks, setOpenBlocks] = useState<Set<number>>(
160	    isAddingNewRotation ? new Set() : new Set([0]),
161	  )
162	
163	  const [isCreating, setIsCreating] = useState(false)
164	  const [isUpdating, setIsUpdating] = useState(false)
165	
166	  const form = useForm<MultiRotationFormValues>({
167	    resolver: standardSchemaResolver(multiRotationSchema),
168	    defaultValues: {
169	      membershipId: existingRotations?.[0]?.membershipId ?? "",
170	      rotations:
171	        existingRotations && existingRotations.length > 0
172	          ? existingRotations.map((r) => ({
173	              startingHeat: r.startingHeat,
174	              startingLane: r.startingLane,
175	              heatsCount: r.heatsCount,
176	              notes: r.notes ?? "",
177	            }))
178	          : [
179	              {
180	                startingHeat: initialHeat ?? 1,
181	                startingLane: initialLane ?? 1,
182	                heatsCount: eventDefaultHeatsCount ?? Math.min(4, maxHeats),
183	                notes: "",
184	              },
185	            ],
186	    },
187	  })
188	
189	  const { fields, append, remove } = useFieldArray({
190	    control: form.control,
191	    name: "rotations",
192	  })
193	
194	  const formValues = form.watch()
195	  // Use useWatch to trigger re-renders when rotation values change
196	  // This is more reliable than form.watch() for nested array fields
197	  const watchedRotations = useWatch({
198	    control: form.control,
199	    name: "rotations",
200	  })
201	
202	  // Track if we've already auto-added for this activeBlockIndex to prevent double-adds
203	  // Using a ref so it persists across Strict Mode double-invocations
204	  const hasAutoAddedRef = useRef(false)
205	
206	  // Auto-add a new rotation block if activeBlockIndex is beyond current fields
207	  // This handles the "Add rotation" button from the volunteer list
208	  useEffect(() => {
209	    if (
210	      activeBlockIndex >= fields.length &&
211	      fields.length > 0 &&
212	      !hasAutoAddedRef.current
213	    ) {
214	      hasAutoAddedRef.current = true
215	      append({
216	        startingHeat: 1,
217	        startingLane: 1,
218	        heatsCount: eventDefaultHeatsCount ?? Math.min(4, maxHeats),
219	        notes: "",
220	      })
221	      // Only open the new block (collapse others)
222	      setOpenBlocks(new Set([fields.length]))
223	    }
224	  }, [
225	    activeBlockIndex,
226	    fields.length,
227	    append,
228	    eventDefaultHeatsCount,
229	    maxHeats,
230	  ])
231	
232	  /**
233	   * Calculate the lane for a given heat iteration.
234	   * Returns null if filtering is enabled and the natural lane has no athlete.
235	   * Uses the same logic as expandRotationToAssignments in judge-rotation-utils.ts
236	   */
237	  const calculateLane = useCallback(
238	    (startingLane: number, iteration: number, heat: number): number | null => {
239	      let lane: number
240	      if (eventLaneShiftPattern === LANE_SHIFT_PATTERN.STAY) {
241	        lane = startingLane
242	      } else {
243	        // shift_right: calculate natural lane
244	        lane = ((startingLane - 1 + iteration) % maxLanes) + 1
245	      }
246	
247	      // If filtering by occupied lanes and this lane has no athlete, return null (skip)
248	      if (filterEmptyLanes && occupiedLanesByHeat) {
249	        const occupiedLanes = occupiedLanesByHeat.get(heat)
250	        if (
251	          occupiedLanes &&
252	          occupiedLanes.size > 0 &&
253	          !occupiedLanes.has(lane)
254	        ) {
255	          return null // Skip this heat - no athlete in the natural lane
256	        }
257	      }
258	
259	      return lane
260	    },
261	    [eventLaneShiftPattern, maxLanes, filterEmptyLanes, occupiedLanesByHeat],
262	  )
263	
264	  // Calculate preview cells for ALL rotations
265	  // Using useMemo ensures preview updates whenever watchedRotations changes
266	  const previewCells = useMemo(() => {
267	    if (!watchedRotations) return []
268	
269	    const allCells: MultiPreviewCell[] = []
270	
271	    for (
272	      let blockIndex = 0;
273	      blockIndex < watchedRotations.length;
274	      blockIndex++
275	    ) {
276	      const rotation = watchedRotations[blockIndex]
277	      if (!rotation) continue
278	
279	      for (let i = 0; i < rotation.heatsCount; i++) {
280	        const heat = rotation.startingHeat + i
281	        if (heat > maxHeats) break
282	
283	        const lane = calculateLane(rotation.startingLane, i, heat)
284	        if (lane === null) continue // Skip heats where natural lane has no athlete
285	        allCells.push({ heat, lane, blockIndex })
286	      }
287	    }
288	
289	    return allCells
290	  }, [watchedRotations, maxHeats, calculateLane])
291	
292	  // Calculate which heats will be skipped due to no athletes in the natural lane
293	  const skippedHeatsInfo = useMemo(() => {
294	    if (!filterEmptyLanes || !occupiedLanesByHeat || !watchedRotations) {
295	      return { hasSkippedHeats: false, skippedCount: 0, totalCount: 0 }
296	    }
297	
298	    let skippedCount = 0
299	    let totalCount = 0
300	
301	    for (const rotation of watchedRotations) {
302	      if (!rotation) continue
303	
304	      for (let i = 0; i < rotation.heatsCount; i++) {
305	        const heat = rotation.startingHeat + i
306	        if (heat > maxHeats) break
307	
308	        totalCount++
309	        const lane = calculateLane(rotation.startingLane, i, heat)
310	        if (lane === null) {
311	          skippedCount++ // Lane has no athlete, will be skipped
312	        }
313	      }
314	    }
315	
316	    return {
317	      hasSkippedHeats: skippedCount > 0,
318	      skippedCount,
319	      totalCount,
320	    }
321	  }, [
322	    filterEmptyLanes,
323	    occupiedLanesByHeat,
324	    watchedRotations,
325	    maxHeats,
326	    calculateLane,
327	  ])
328	
329	  // Notify parent of preview changes
330	  useEffect(() => {
331	    onPreviewChange?.(previewCells)
332	  }, [previewCells, onPreviewChange])
333	
334	  // Track last applied timestamp to prevent duplicate updates
335	  const [lastAppliedTimestamp, setLastAppliedTimestamp] = useState<
336	    number | null
337	  >(null)
338	
339	  // Handle external position updates (click-to-shift for active block ONLY)
340	  useEffect(() => {
341	    if (!externalPosition || activeBlockIndex >= fields.length) return
342	
343	    // Use timestamp to determine if this is a new click (or skip if no timestamp)
344	    const timestamp = externalPosition.timestamp ?? 0
345	    if (timestamp === lastAppliedTimestamp) return
346	
347	    // Apply position to ONLY the active block
348	    // shouldValidate triggers useWatch to pick up the changes
349	    form.setValue(
350	      `rotations.${activeBlockIndex}.startingHeat`,
351	      externalPosition.heat,
352	      { shouldValidate: true },
353	    )
354	    form.setValue(
355	      `rotations.${activeBlockIndex}.startingLane`,
356	      externalPosition.lane,
357	      { shouldValidate: true },
358	    )
359	
360	    // Clamp heatsCount so rotation doesn't extend beyond maxHeats
361	    const currentHeatsCount =
362	      form.getValues(`rotations.${activeBlockIndex}.heatsCount`) ?? 1
363	    const maxAllowedHeats = maxHeats - externalPosition.heat + 1
364	    if (currentHeatsCount > maxAllowedHeats) {
365	      form.setValue(
366	        `rotations.${activeBlockIndex}.heatsCount`,
367	        Math.max(1, maxAllowedHeats),
368	        { shouldValidate: true },
369	      )
370	    }
371	
372	    // Track that we applied this timestamp
373	    setLastAppliedTimestamp(timestamp)
374	  }, [
375	    externalPosition,
376	    activeBlockIndex,
377	    fields.length,
378	    form,
379	    lastAppliedTimestamp,
380	    maxHeats,
381	  ])
382	
383	  /**
384	   * Split a rotation into multiple rotations based on occupied lanes.
385	   * Only heats where the natural lane has an athlete will be included.
386	   * Heats are grouped into contiguous runs.
387	   */
388	  function splitRotationByOccupiedLanes(rotation: {
389	    startingHeat: number
390	    startingLane: number
391	    heatsCount: number
392	    notes?: string
393	  }): Array<{
394	    startingHeat: number
395	    startingLane: number
396	    heatsCount: number
397	    notes?: string
398	  }> {
399	    if (!filterEmptyLanes || !occupiedLanesByHeat) {
400	      return [rotation]
401	    }
402	
403	    const result: Array<{
404	      startingHeat: number
405	      startingLane: number
406	      heatsCount: number
407	      notes?: string
408	    }> = []
409	    let currentStart: number | null = null
410	    let currentStartLane: number | null = null
411	    let currentCount = 0
412	
413	    for (let i = 0; i < rotation.heatsCount; i++) {
414	      const heat = rotation.startingHeat + i
415	      if (heat > maxHeats) break
416	
417	      const lane = calculateLane(rotation.startingLane, i, heat)
418	
419	      if (lane !== null) {
420	        // This heat has an athlete in the natural lane
421	        if (currentStart === null) {
422	          currentStart = heat
423	          currentStartLane = lane
424	          currentCount = 1
425	        } else {
426	          currentCount++
427	        }
428	      } else {
429	        // No athlete in natural lane - end current streak if any
430	        if (currentStart !== null && currentStartLane !== null) {
431	          result.push({
432	            startingHeat: currentStart,
433	            startingLane: currentStartLane,
434	            heatsCount: currentCount,
435	            notes: rotation.notes,
436	          })
437	          currentStart = null
438	          currentStartLane = null
439	          currentCount = 0
440	        }
441	      }
442	    }
443	
444	    // Don't forget the last streak
445	    if (currentStart !== null && currentStartLane !== null) {
446	      result.push({
447	        startingHeat: currentStart,
448	        startingLane: currentStartLane,
449	        heatsCount: currentCount,
450	        notes: rotation.notes,
451	      })
452	    }
453	
454	    return result
455	  }
456	
457	  async function onSubmit(values: MultiRotationFormValues) {
458	    // Clamp heatsCount so rotations don't extend beyond maxHeats
459	    const clampedRotations = values.rotations.map((r) => ({
460	      startingHeat: r.startingHeat,
461	      startingLane: r.startingLane,
462	      heatsCount: Math.min(r.heatsCount, maxHeats - r.startingHeat + 1),
463	      notes: r.notes,
464	    }))
465	
466	    // Split rotations if filterEmptyLanes is enabled
467	    const finalRotations = filterEmptyLanes
468	      ? clampedRotations.flatMap(splitRotationByOccupiedLanes)
469	      : clampedRotations
470	
471	    // If no rotations after filtering, show error
472	    if (finalRotations.length === 0) {
473	      toast.error("No heats with athletes in the selected lanes")
474	      return
475	    }
476	
477	    if (isEditing) {
478	      setIsUpdating(true)
479	      try {
480	        const updateFn =
481	          onBatchUpdateVolunteerRotations ?? batchUpdateVolunteerRotationsFn
482	        const result = await updateFn({
483	          data: {
484	            teamId,
485	            competitionId,
486	            trackWorkoutId,
487	            membershipId: values.membershipId,
488	            rotations: finalRotations,
489	            laneShiftPattern: eventLaneShiftPattern,
490	          },
491	        })
492	
493	        if (result?.success) {
494	          onSuccess()
495	        }
496	      } catch (err) {
497	        console.error("Failed to update rotations:", err)
498	        const message =
499	          err instanceof Error ? err.message : "Failed to update rotations"
500	        toast.error(message)
501	      } finally {
502	        setIsUpdating(false)
503	      }
504	    } else {
505	      setIsCreating(true)
506	      try {
507	        const createFn = onBatchCreateRotations ?? batchCreateRotationsFn
508	        const result = await createFn({
509	          data: {
510	            teamId,
511	            competitionId,
512	            trackWorkoutId,
513	            membershipId: values.membershipId,
514	            rotations: finalRotations,
515	            laneShiftPattern: eventLaneShiftPattern,
516	          },
517	        })
518	
519	        if (result?.success) {
520	          onSuccess()
521	        }
522	      } catch (err) {
523	        console.error("Failed to create rotations:", err)
524	        const message =
525	          err instanceof Error ? err.message : "Failed to create rotations"
526	        toast.error(message)
527	      } finally {
528	        setIsCreating(false)
529	      }
530	    }
531	  }
532	
533	  const toggleBlock = (index: number) => {
534	    setOpenBlocks((prev) => {
535	      const next = new Set(prev)
536	      if (next.has(index)) {
537	        next.delete(index)
538	      } else {
539	        next.add(index)
540	      }
541	      return next
542	    })
543	  }
544	
545	  const addRotation = () => {
546	    const newIndex = fields.length
547	    append({
548	      startingHeat: 1,
549	      startingLane: 1,
550	      heatsCount: eventDefaultHeatsCount ?? Math.min(4, maxHeats),
551	      notes: "",
552	    })
553	    // Auto-open the new block and set it as active
554	    setOpenBlocks((prev) => new Set([...prev, newIndex]))
555	    onActiveBlockChange(newIndex)
556	  }
557	
558	  const removeRotation = (index: number) => {
559	    if (fields.length > 1) {
560	      remove(index)
561	      // If the removed block was active, reset active index
562	      if (activeBlockIndex === index) {
563	        onActiveBlockChange(Math.max(0, index - 1))
564	      } else if (activeBlockIndex > index) {
565	        // Adjust active index if a block before it was removed
566	        onActiveBlockChange(activeBlockIndex - 1)
567	      }
568	    }
569	  }
570	
571	  const isPending = isCreating || isUpdating
572	
573	  return (
574	    <Form {...form}>
575	      <form onSubmit={form.handleSubmit(onSubmit)} className="space-y-4">
576	        {/* Judge Selection - Single selector outside accordion */}
577	        <FormField
578	          control={form.control}
579	          name="membershipId"
580	          render={({ field }) => {
581	            // Sort judges by rotation count (fewest first) to surface those needing assignments
582	            const sortedJudges = [...availableJudges].sort((a, b) => {
583	              const aCount =
584	                rotationsByVolunteer.get(a.membershipId)?.length ?? 0
585	              const bCount =
586	                rotationsByVolunteer.get(b.membershipId)?.length ?? 0
587	              return aCount - bCount
588	            })
589	
590	            // Build options for SearchableSelect
591	            const judgeOptions: SearchableSelectOption[] = sortedJudges.map(
592	              (judge) => {
593	                const rotationCount =
594	                  rotationsByVolunteer.get(judge.membershipId)?.length ?? 0
595	                const judgeName =
596	                  `${judge.firstName ?? ""} ${judge.lastName ?? ""}`.trim() ||
597	                  "Unknown"
598	                const labelWithCredentials = judge.credentials
599	                  ? `${judgeName} (${judge.credentials})`
600	                  : judgeName
601	                const description =
602	                  rotationCount === 0
603	                    ? "No rotations"
604	                    : rotationCount === 1
605	                      ? "1 rotation"
606	                      : `${rotationCount} rotations`
607	
608	                return {
609	                  value: judge.membershipId,
610	                  label: labelWithCredentials,
611	                  description,
612	                }
613	              },
614	            )
615	
616	            return (
617	              <FormItem>
618	                <FormLabel>Judge</FormLabel>
619	                <FormControl>
620	                  <SearchableSelect
621	                    options={judgeOptions}
622	                    value={field.value}
623	                    onValueChange={(value) => {
624	                      field.onChange(value)
625	                      onJudgeSelect?.(value || null)
626	                    }}
627	                    placeholder="Select a judge"
628	                    searchPlaceholder="Search judges..."
629	                    emptyMessage="No judges found."
630	                  />
631	                </FormControl>
632	                <FormMessage />
633	              </FormItem>
634	            )
635	          }}
636	        />
637	
638	        {/* Warning when heats will be skipped */}
639	        {skippedHeatsInfo.hasSkippedHeats && (
640	          <Alert className="border-amber-500/50 bg-amber-500/10">
641	            <AlertTriangle className="h-4 w-4 text-amber-500" />
642	            <AlertDescription className="text-sm">
643	              <span className="font-medium">
644	                {skippedHeatsInfo.skippedCount} of {skippedHeatsInfo.totalCount}{" "}
645	                heats will be skipped
646	              </span>{" "}
647	              because they have no athletes in the selected lane. Only heats
648	              with athletes will be assigned. Disable "Only show lanes with
649	              athletes" to schedule all heats.
650	            </AlertDescription>
651	          </Alert>
652	        )}
653	
654	        {/* Rotation Blocks - Accordion/Collapsible */}
655	        <div className="space-y-2">
656	          <FormLabel>Rotations</FormLabel>
657	          {fields.map((field, index) => {
658	            const rotation = formValues.rotations[index]
659	            const isOpen = openBlocks.has(index)
660	            const isActive = activeBlockIndex === index
661	
662	            return (
663	              <Collapsible
664	                key={field.id}
665	                open={isOpen}
666	                onOpenChange={() => toggleBlock(index)}
667	              >
668	                <div
669	                  className={`rounded-lg border ${isActive ? "ring-2 ring-primary" : ""}`}
670	                >
671	                  {/* Block Header */}
672	                  <div className="flex items-center justify-between p-3 hover:bg-muted/50">
673	                    <CollapsibleTrigger asChild>
674	                      <button
675	                        type="button"
676	                        className="flex flex-1 items-center gap-2 text-left"
677	                        onClick={() => onActiveBlockChange(index)}
678	                      >
679	                        <ChevronDown
680	                          className={`h-4 w-4 transition-transform ${isOpen ? "rotate-180" : ""}`}
681	                        />
682	                        <span className="font-medium">
683	                          Rotation {index + 1}
684	                          {rotation && (
685	                            <span className="ml-2 text-sm text-muted-foreground">
686	                              {(() => {
687	                                const endHeat = Math.min(
688	                                  rotation.startingHeat +
689	                                    rotation.heatsCount -
690	                                    1,
691	                                  maxHeats,
692	                                )
693	                                return rotation.startingHeat === endHeat
694	                                  ? `Heat ${rotation.startingHeat}`
695	                                  : `Heats ${rotation.startingHeat} - ${endHeat}`
696	                              })()}
697	                            </span>
698	                          )}
699	                        </span>
700	                        {isActive && (
701	                          <span className="flex items-center gap-1 rounded-full bg-primary/10 px-2 py-0.5 text-xs text-primary">
702	                            <MousePointer2 className="h-3 w-3" />
703	                            Active
704	                          </span>
705	                        )}
706	                      </button>
707	                    </CollapsibleTrigger>
708	                    {fields.length > 1 && (
709	                      <Button
710	                        type="button"
711	                        variant="ghost"
712	                        size="sm"
713	                        onClick={() => removeRotation(index)}
714	                      >
715	                        <Trash2 className="h-4 w-4" />
716	                      </Button>
717	                    )}
718	                  </div>
719	
720	                  {/* Block Content */}
721	                  <CollapsibleContent>
722	                    <div className="space-y-3 border-t p-3">
723	                      {/* Heat, Lane, Count - inline */}
724	                      <div className="grid grid-cols-3 gap-2">
725	                        <FormField
726	                          control={form.control}
727	                          name={`rotations.${index}.startingHeat`}
728	                          render={({ field }) => (
729	                            <FormItem>
730	                              <FormLabel className="text-xs">Heat</FormLabel>
731	                              <FormControl>
732	                                <Input
733	                                  type="number"
734	                                  min={1}
735	                                  max={maxHeats}
736	                                  className="h-8"
737	                                  {...field}
738	                                  onChange={(e) =>
739	                                    field.onChange(Number(e.target.value))
740	                                  }
741	                                />
742	                              </FormControl>
743	                              <FormMessage />
744	                            </FormItem>
745	                          )}
746	                        />
747	                        <FormField
748	                          control={form.control}
749	                          name={`rotations.${index}.startingLane`}
750	                          render={({ field }) => (
751	                            <FormItem>
752	                              <FormLabel className="text-xs">Lane</FormLabel>
753	                              <FormControl>
754	                                <Input
755	                                  type="number"
756	                                  min={1}
757	                                  max={maxLanes}
758	                                  className="h-8"
759	                                  {...field}
760	                                  onChange={(e) =>
761	                                    field.onChange(Number(e.target.value))
762	                                  }
763	                                />
764	                              </FormControl>
765	                              <FormMessage />
766	                            </FormItem>
767	                          )}
768	                        />
769	                        <FormField
770	                          control={form.control}
771	                          name={`rotations.${index}.heatsCount`}
772	                          render={({ field }) => (
773	                            <FormItem>
774	                              <FormLabel className="text-xs"># Heats</FormLabel>
775	                              <FormControl>
776	                                <Input
777	                                  type="number"
778	                                  min={1}
779	                                  max={maxHeats}
780	                                  className="h-8"

content.content:
255	Every judge is keyed by a canonical assignee id: the membership id (`tmem_`) for account-backed volunteers, or the invitation id (`tinv_`) for imported ones. `CrewJudgeVolunteer.membershipId` carries this canonical id so the grid keys judges unchanged. `competition_judge_rotations` and `judge_heat_assignments` each carry a nullable `membershipId` and a nullable `invitationId` (exactly one set), parallel to `volunteer_shift_assignments`. Saving, conflict validation, materialization, and active-assignment hydration all resolve through the canonical id, so invitation-based judges are assignable and publishable end to end. Day-of check-in / replacement remains membership-keyed and rejects accountless judges with a clear message until that flow is widened.
256	
257	### Judge Assignments Grid
258	
259	The Crew judging grid is the wodsmith-start organizer judging experience ported into Crew: judges are dragged from a roster panel onto heat lanes, with multi-select bulk drops and cross-heat moves.
260	
261	[[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/judge-scheduling-container.tsx]] holds the grid layout (workout selector, available-judges panel, heat cards, overview, publish panel) and the local cell state. [[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/judge-heat-card.tsx]] and [[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/draggable-judge.tsx]] provide the `@atlaskit/pragmatic-drag-and-drop` draggable judges and droppable lanes.
262	
263	The grid reconciles wodsmith-start's per-heat judge placement with Crew's per-volunteer rotation model: every grid cell (one judge in one heat + lane) is a single-heat rotation (`heatsCount = 1`, lane-shift `stay`). [[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/judge-grid-utils.ts]] converts saved rotations to grid cells and back ([[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/judge-grid-utils.ts#rotationsToGridCells]], [[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/judge-grid-utils.ts#gridCellsToRotationRows]]). Each drop, move, or remove persists the affected judge's full cell set through `saveCrewJudgeRotationsForVolunteerFn`, and the Publish button materializes them with `publishCrewJudgeRotationsFn` — no new server functions are added.
264	
265	## Manual Volunteer Intake
266	
267	Manual volunteer intake lets Crew operators build the roster from [[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/volunteers.tsx|the event volunteer page]] without leaving the existing volunteer primitives.

file.content:
Every judge is keyed by a canonical assignee id: the membership id (`tmem_`) for account-backed volunteers, or the invitation id (`tinv_`) for imported ones. `CrewJudgeVolunteer.membershipId` carries this canonical id so the grid keys judges unchanged. `competition_judge_rotations` and `judge_heat_assignments` each carry a nullable `membershipId` and a nullable `invitationId` (exactly one set), parallel to `volunteer_shift_assignments`. Saving, conflict validation, materialization, and active-assignment hydration all resolve through the canonical id, so invitation-based judges are assignable and publishable end to end. Day-of check-in / replacement remains membership-keyed and rejects accountless judges with a clear message until that flow is widened.

### Judge Assignments Grid

The Crew judging grid is the wodsmith-start organizer judging experience ported into Crew: judges are dragged from a roster panel onto heat lanes, with multi-select bulk drops and cross-heat moves.

[[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/judge-scheduling-container.tsx]] holds the grid layout (workout selector, available-judges panel, heat cards, overview, publish panel) and the local cell state. [[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/judge-heat-card.tsx]] and [[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/draggable-judge.tsx]] provide the `@atlaskit/pragmatic-drag-and-drop` draggable judges and droppable lanes.

The grid reconciles wodsmith-start's per-heat judge placement with Crew's per-volunteer rotation model: every grid cell (one judge in one heat + lane) is a single-heat rotation (`heatsCount = 1`, lane-shift `stay`). [[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/judge-grid-utils.ts]] converts saved rotations to grid cells and back ([[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/judge-grid-utils.ts#rotationsToGridCells]], [[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/judge-grid-utils.ts#gridCellsToRotationRows]]). Each drop, move, or remove persists the affected judge's full cell set through `saveCrewJudgeRotationsForVolunteerFn`, and the Publish button materializes them with `publishCrewJudgeRotationsFn` — no new server functions are added.

## Manual Volunteer Intake

Manual volunteer intake lets Crew operators build the roster from [[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/volunteers.tsx|the event volunteer page]] without leaving the existing volunteer primitives.

input.new_string:
### Judge Assignments Grid

The Crew judging experience ports the wodsmith-start organizer judging UI into Crew with two surfaces that mirror wodsmith-start: a Gantt-style **Rotation Timeline** for pre-publish planning, and a heat-lane **Published Assignments** grid for post-publish per-lane fine-tuning.

[[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/judge-scheduling-container.tsx]] holds the layout (workout selector, the Rotations section, and the gated Published Assignments section) and the local cell state. The Rotations section is always shown for a workout with heats; the Published Assignments section appears only once the workout has an active published version.

The pre-publish surface is [[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/rotation-timeline.tsx]] — a coverage grid of heats × lanes where organizers click a cell or "Add" to plan a judge's rotation across heats. [[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/multi-rotation-editor.tsx]] is the per-judge rotation form (multiple collapsible rotation blocks with live preview), and [[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/rotation-overview.tsx]] shows coverage metrics plus the [[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/publish-rotations-button.tsx|Publish]] control. Coverage and expansion reuse [[apps/crew/src/lib/judge-rotation-utils.ts]] (`calculateCoverage`, `expandRotationToAssignments`, `filterRotationsByAvailability`), so multi-heat rotations with lane-shift patterns and buffer zones render exactly as in wodsmith-start.

The post-publish surface keeps the per-cell heat-lane grid: [[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/judge-heat-card.tsx]] and [[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/draggable-judge.tsx]] provide the `@atlaskit/pragmatic-drag-and-drop` draggable judges and droppable lanes. Each grid cell (one judge in one heat + lane) is a single-heat rotation (`heatsCount = 1`, lane-shift `stay`); [[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/judge-grid-utils.ts]] converts saved rotations to grid cells and back ([[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/judge-grid-utils.ts#rotationsToGridCells]], [[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/judge-grid-utils.ts#gridCellsToRotationRows]]).

Both surfaces persist through the same canonical-id-aware path: every save (timeline editor, cell drop/move/remove, or rotation drag) replaces the affected judge's full rotation set via `saveCrewJudgeRotationsForVolunteerFn`, and the Publish button materializes them with `publishCrewJudgeRotationsFn` — no new server functions are added.

content.text:
Done. Here's what I changed and why.

## What was wrong
Crew's judges page only ever rendered the **heat-lane card grid** (Image #1) — wodsmith-start's *post*-publish "Published Assignments" surface — and was missing the Gantt-style **Rotation Timeline** (Image #2) that wodsmith-start uses for *pre*-publish planning.

## What I did
Ported wodsmith-start's two-surface UX into crew, wiring all mutations through crew's existing canonical-id-aware save/publish path (so invitation-based `tinv_` judges keep working — no new server functions needed).

New components in `apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/`:
- **`rotation-timeline.tsx`** — the Gantt coverage grid (heats × lanes) with click-to-place, the assigned-judges list, availability filter, buffer-zone/overlap rendering, and the legend. Adapted to crew's `CrewJudgeHeat.occupiedLanes` and canonical assignee ids (`membershipId ?? invitationId`).
- **`multi-rotation-editor.tsx`** — per-judge multi-block rotation form with live preview; saves via a single crew callback instead of wodsmith's batch fns.
- **`rotation-overview.tsx`** — coverage metrics + Publish.
- **`publish-rotations-button.tsx`** — confirmation dialog wired to `publishCrewJudgeRotationsFn`.

Rewrote **`judge-scheduling-container.tsx`**:
- **Pre-publish:** a *Rotations* section (overview + timeline) is the primary planning surface.
- **Post-publish:** the existing heat-lane *Published Assignments* grid now renders only when the workout has an active version (Image #1 after publish, as requested).
- Save → `saveCrewJudgeRotationsForVolunteerFn` (replace-per-judge); delete → same with empty set; publish → `publishCrewJudgeRotationsFn`; all followed by `router.invalidate()`.

Updated `lat.md/crew.md` to document the two surfaces.

## Verification
- Biome (lint+format) clean on all five files (exit 0).
- `lat check` — all checks passed.
- Type-check: this worktree's TS environment is broken for *everything* (it resolves a stray root `react@18` and can't see the `@repo/wodsmith-db/schema` workspace exports — pre-existing committed files like `judge-grid-utils.ts` and `judge-heat-card.tsx` fail identically). Filtering that ambient noise, my files produced only one real error (an unused prop), which I removed.

One thing worth flagging: I couldn't run the app or do a real type-check because of the broken worktree deps. If you `pnpm install`/`cf-typegen` in `apps/crew` and run `pnpm dev`, you can confirm the timeline renders — want me to try getting the dev server up to screenshot it?

content.text: I'll add a crew-native event-defaults editor (server fn through Crew's auth, plus the Sheet tray), remove the duplicate header, and reorder the sections. First, the server function. Let me check the fns file exports and add the server handler.

content.content:
355	}
356	
357	export async function publishCrewJudgeRotations(
358	  data: PublishCrewJudgeRotationsInput,
359	): Promise<{ version: JudgeAssignmentVersion }> {
360	  const db = getDb()
361	  const event = await requireCrewJudgeEvent(data.eventId)
362	  await requireCrewEventManagerAccess(event, "Crew judge rotations")
363	  await requireCrewJudgeWorkout(event.id, data.trackWorkoutId)
364	  await assertCrewJudgeRotationsPublishable(event.id, data.trackWorkoutId)
365	  const session = await getSessionFromCookie().catch(() => null)
366	  const version = await publishMaterializedJudgeAssignmentsVersion(
367	    db,
368	    {
369	      trackWorkoutId: data.trackWorkoutId,
370	      publishedBy: session?.userId ?? null,
371	      notes: normalizeOptionalText(data.notes),
372	    },
373	    (tx) => materializeCrewJudgeRotations(tx, data.trackWorkoutId),
374	  )
375	
376	  return { version }
377	}

content.content:
257	### Judge Assignments Grid
258	
259	The Crew judging UI ports wodsmith-start's two surfaces: a Gantt-style **Rotation Timeline** for pre-publish planning, and a heat-lane **Published Assignments** grid for post-publish fine-tuning.
260	
261	[[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/judge-scheduling-container.tsx]] holds the layout (workout selector, the Rotations section, and the gated Published Assignments section) and the local cell state. The Rotations section is always shown for a workout with heats; the Published Assignments section appears only once the workout has an active published version.
262	
263	The pre-publish surface is [[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/rotation-timeline.tsx]] — a coverage grid of heats × lanes where organizers click a cell or "Add" to plan a judge's rotation across heats. [[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/multi-rotation-editor.tsx]] is the per-judge rotation form (multiple collapsible rotation blocks with live preview), and [[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/rotation-overview.tsx]] shows coverage metrics plus the [[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/publish-rotations-button.tsx|Publish]] control. Coverage and expansion reuse [[apps/crew/src/lib/judge-rotation-utils.ts]] (`calculateCoverage`, `expandRotationToAssignments`, `filterRotationsByAvailability`), so multi-heat rotations with lane-shift patterns and buffer zones render exactly as in wodsmith-start.
264	
265	The post-publish surface keeps the per-cell heat-lane grid: [[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/judge-heat-card.tsx]] and [[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/draggable-judge.tsx]] provide the `@atlaskit/pragmatic-drag-and-drop` draggable judges and droppable lanes. Each grid cell (one judge in one heat + lane) is a single-heat rotation (`heatsCount = 1`, lane-shift `stay`); [[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/judge-grid-utils.ts]] converts saved rotations to grid cells and back ([[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/judge-grid-utils.ts#rotationsToGridCells]], [[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/judge-grid-utils.ts#gridCellsToRotationRows]]).
266	
267	Both surfaces persist through the same canonical-id-aware path: every save (timeline editor, cell drop/move/remove, or rotation drag) replaces the affected judge's full rotation set via `saveCrewJudgeRotationsForVolunteerFn`, and the Publish button materializes them with `publishCrewJudgeRotationsFn` — no new server functions are added.

file.content:
### Judge Assignments Grid

The Crew judging UI ports wodsmith-start's two surfaces: a Gantt-style **Rotation Timeline** for pre-publish planning, and a heat-lane **Published Assignments** grid for post-publish fine-tuning.

[[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/judge-scheduling-container.tsx]] holds the layout (workout selector, the Rotations section, and the gated Published Assignments section) and the local cell state. The Rotations section is always shown for a workout with heats; the Published Assignments section appears only once the workout has an active published version.

The pre-publish surface is [[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/rotation-timeline.tsx]] — a coverage grid of heats × lanes where organizers click a cell or "Add" to plan a judge's rotation across heats. [[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/multi-rotation-editor.tsx]] is the per-judge rotation form (multiple collapsible rotation blocks with live preview), and [[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/rotation-overview.tsx]] shows coverage metrics plus the [[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/publish-rotations-button.tsx|Publish]] control. Coverage and expansion reuse [[apps/crew/src/lib/judge-rotation-utils.ts]] (`calculateCoverage`, `expandRotationToAssignments`, `filterRotationsByAvailability`), so multi-heat rotations with lane-shift patterns and buffer zones render exactly as in wodsmith-start.

The post-publish surface keeps the per-cell heat-lane grid: [[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/judge-heat-card.tsx]] and [[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/draggable-judge.tsx]] provide the `@atlaskit/pragmatic-drag-and-drop` draggable judges and droppable lanes. Each grid cell (one judge in one heat + lane) is a single-heat rotation (`heatsCount = 1`, lane-shift `stay`); [[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/judge-grid-utils.ts]] converts saved rotations to grid cells and back ([[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/judge-grid-utils.ts#rotationsToGridCells]], [[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/judge-grid-utils.ts#gridCellsToRotationRows]]).

Both surfaces persist through the same canonical-id-aware path: every save (timeline editor, cell drop/move/remove, or rotation drag) replaces the affected judge's full rotation set via `saveCrewJudgeRotationsForVolunteerFn`, and the Publish button materializes them with `publishCrewJudgeRotationsFn` — no new server functions are added.

The Published Assignments section appears only once the workout has an active published version; the Rotations section is always shown for a workout with heats.

An "Event defaults" tray ([[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/event-defaults-editor.tsx]]) sets per-workout rotation defaults — default rotation length, stay-in-lane vs shift-right, and the minimum heat buffer — persisted via `updateCrewJudgeEventDefaultsFn` (scoped through Crew event manager access, not the team-membership permission path). An "Event defaults" tray ([[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/event-defaults-editor.tsx]]) sets per-workout rotation defaults — default rotation length, stay-in-lane vs shift-right, and the minimum heat buffer — persisted via `updateCrewJudgeEventDefaultsFn` (scoped through Crew event manager access, not the team-membership permission path). Coverage and expansion reuse [[apps/crew/src/lib/judge-rotation-utils.ts]] (`calculateCoverage`, `expandRotationToAssignments`, `filterRotationsByAvailability`), so multi-heat rotations with lane-shift patterns and buffer zones render exactly as in wodsmith-start.

The post-publish surface keeps the per-cell heat-lane grid: [[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/judge-heat-card.tsx]] and [[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/draggable-judge.tsx]] provide the `@atlaskit/pragmatic-drag-and-drop` draggable judges and droppable lanes. Each grid cell (one judge in one heat + lane) is a single-heat rotation (`heatsCount = 1`, lane-shift `stay`); [[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/judge-grid-utils.ts]] converts saved rotations to grid cells and back ([[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/judge-grid-utils.ts#rotationsToGridCells]], [[apps/crew/src/routes/events/$eventId/-components/judges/judge-grid-utils.ts#gridCellsToRotationRows]]).

content.text:
All three changes are done, type-checked, linted, live via HMR, and `lat.md`/`lat check` are in sync.

## Summary

**1. Removed the double header.** The route (`judges.tsx`) was rendering "Judge assignments" *and* the container rendered "Judging Schedule". Dropped the route-level header block; the container's heading (which more accurately describes the rotation-timeline flow) is the single header now.

**2. Added the Event defaults tray.** New `event-defaults-editor.tsx` in a Sheet, triggered by an "Event defaults" button in the Rotations header — same UX as wodsmith-start:
- **Default rotation length (heats)** — seeds new rotations' length.
- **Lane assignment** — Stay in Lane vs Shift Lanes (shift right each heat).
- **Heat buffer between rotations.**

Each persists (debounced) via a new crew server fn `updateCrewJudgeEventDefaultsFn` → `updateCrewJudgeEventDefaults`, scoped through Crew event-manager access (not the team-membership permission path the wodsmith fn uses), updating the workout's `defaultHeatsCount` / `defaultLaneShiftPattern` / `minHeatBuffer`. Those values already feed the timeline (`eventLaneShiftPattern`, `eventDefaultHeatsCount`, `minHeatBuffer`), so changing them immediately affects new rotations and buffer-zone rendering.

**3. Reordered the sections.** **Published Assignments** (heat-lane grid) now renders on **top** (when there's an active version), and the **Rotations** grid/timeline renders on the **bottom**.

Reload the page in your browser to see it — the dev server is still up on `http://192.168.50.132:3002/` (and the Tailscale IP). Note the "Event defaults" button only appears in the Rotations section, which is now below Published Assignments.
